name: Luis Russell
abstract: Luis Carl Russell (August 5, 1902 – December 11, 1963) was a pioneering Panamanian jazz pianist, orchestra leader, composer, and arranger.

works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: I think probably this one Henry “Red” Allen... it was really the Luis Russell Band but he fronted it and made these wonderful Victor sides. And I grew up with the LP version.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: we’d listen to the old Louis Armstrong records, the “Hot Five”s and the “Hot Seven”s and all of the things he made with Luis Russell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Luis Russell’s band. ... I joined Luis’s band 1945.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Ramey
about_quotes.evidence: Luis Russell came through. He had lost his bass player... they asked me at the last moment if I would go with them... I went with them

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Luis Russell was the bandleader, and in the band was Paul Barbarin, Red Allen, Charlie Holmes, and Luis Russell playing piano

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Luis Russell?' and the interviewee responds 'No, no, no, no. Way before that', indicating a denial of the specific connection or timeline proposed by the interviewer without providing further personal details.
